differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinemia) secondary to chronic acetaminophen and malnutrition
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas with serum lactate, beta-hydroxybutyrate, and anion gap calculation
Order serum acetaminophen level and consider urine organic acid analysis for 5-oxoproline
Perform CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis to evaluate for urothelial cancer progression, obstruction, and possible abdominal source of sepsis
Evaluate medication list to discontinue potentially precipitating agents (e.g., metformin, SGLT2 inhibitors)
Obtain arterial blood gas to assess for chronic CO2 retention and metabolic compensation
Perform blood and urine cultures to guide targeted antimicrobial therapy
